headline: Broadcom Enters Earnings As Citi's Top Semiconductor Pick
summary: The question surrounding Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) heading into its next earnings report is not whether the company belongs among the AI trade’s biggest winners. That debate ended a long time ago. The more pressing question is whether a business already generating tens of billions in AI-related revenue can keep growing fast enough to justify a valuation that has briefly crossed $2 trillion. Citi’s answer, for now, is yes. Analyst Atif Malik raised his price target on the stock from $475 to $500 on

headline: Nokia (NOK) Reset: Is the Infrastructure Giant Still Undervalued At $13?
summary: For years, Nokia stock was dismissed as a legacy hardware player trapped in a low-margin 5G cycle. But in early 2026, the narrative has fundamentally shifted. While the market remained fixated on software giants, Nokia quietly engineered a structural breakout into the $11 to $13 range, fueled by an "AI super-cycle" that is rapidly transforming the telecommunications sector.

headline: Blackstone’s May Portfolio Shuffle Reshapes Risk And Future Fee Potential
summary: In May 2026, Blackstone (NYSE:BX) announced the sale of Recognition Music Group to Sony Music and a lender-led restructuring of Medallia. The firm also agreed to acquire a majority stake in Greek e-commerce platform Skroutz and arranged a financing package with Broadcom tied to AI chip development. Additional moves included a large life sciences investment in Anagram Therapeutics and the launch of a real estate lending platform focused on affordable housing projects. Blackstone is putting...
